payload.title: B82 Southern Brooklyn Select Bus Service
payload.description: For presentation to CB 15 Transportation Committee, presenting proposed B82 SBS designs along the Southern Brooklyn B82 corridor

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.project_name: B82 Southern Brooklyn Select Bus Service
payload.description: Conversion of B82 Limited to B82 Select Bus Service along the Southern Brooklyn B82 corridor, serving 32,000 daily riders, connecting to D N F B Q L subway lines and 21 bus routes
Proposed B82 SBS replacing the B82 Limited, serving same stops as existing B82 Limited within CB15, operating for more hours of the day including mid-day and evenings
Existing B82 Local service; all current stops will still be served by the B82 Local after SBS conversion
Existing B82 Limited service which will be converted to B82 SBS; existing Limited service does not operate mid-day or in the evenings
B7 Local bus route serving Kings Highway and Southern Brooklyn; 12,500 people in CB15 use B82 Limited, B82 Local, or B7 service each day

payload.corridor_name: Kings Highway (Bay Pkwy to Ocean Ave)
payload.description: Commercial/shopping corridor, narrow roadway with one lane in each direction, major east-west transit corridor used by B82 Local, B82 Limited, B7 Local, and other non-MTA buses, with 3 subway stations (B/Q, F, N), local truck route

payload.corridor_name: Kings Highway (E 23 St to Ave K)
payload.description: Wide, boulevard style portion of Kings Highway with narrow, deteriorating medians serving as bus stops, large complex intersections, major east-west transit corridor used by B82 Local, B82 Limited, B7 Local, school buses, coach buses, other buses

B82 corridor in Southern Brooklyn, identified as potential candidate for Select Bus Service in the 2009 Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study; serves 32,000 daily riders, connects to D N F B Q L subway lines and 21 bus routes including B44 Nostrand SBS and B46 Utica SBS
Borough Hall Kickoff Meeting for B82 SBS project
Online Feedback Portal launches for B82 SBS; over 130 location specific comments to date, site still live at nycdotfeedbackportals.nyc
Present draft plan to community boards and elected officials; continue stakeholder outreach

Update street design based on community feedback; begin implementation for critical safety and transit accessibility locations including reconstruct medians at Kings Hwy at Nostrand Ave & Flatbush Ave
Present final plan to community boards and elected officials; continue stakeholder outreach
Implement project street designs, SBS stop amenities; Launch B82 Southern Brooklyn Select Bus Service
Curbside bus lanes on Kings Highway serving all buses, emergency vehicles, and right turns

payload.description: Daily transfers from B82 to B-Q subway
Daily transfers from B82 to F subway
SBS has brought 10-30% faster bus speeds
SBS has brought about 10% increase in ridership
Customer satisfaction of 95% for SBS routes

payload.claim_text: 85% of businesses report typical customer visit is 1 hour or less
payload.subject: Kings Highway businesses customer visit duration

payload.claim_text: 67% of businesses report double parking interferes with deliveries
payload.subject: Kings Highway businesses delivery problems

payload.claim_text: 46% of businesses report vehicles at curb interfere with deliveries
payload.subject: Kings Highway businesses delivery problems

payload.claim_text: 96% of businesses have no off-street parking for customers or deliveries; 3% for customers and deliveries; 1% for customers only; 0% offer employee parking
payload.subject: Kings Highway businesses off-street parking

payload.claim_text: Weekday mode split on Kings Highway: Walk 51%, Transit 24%, Car 23%. Sub-mode: Bus 11%, Subway 13%
Weekend mode split on Kings Highway: Walk 58%, Transit 19%, Car 21%. Sub-mode: Bus 8%, Subway 11%

Large majority of people coming to Kings Highway arrive without a car, either on foot or by transit. For those who drive, parking is on-street, in demand particularly 12-7 PM, short visits per business (< 1 hr), availability limited by meter-feeding throughout day. Deliveries are common, often short, occur most frequently 10 AM - 2 PM, in small trucks or vans, often hindered by parking/double parking activity. About half of businesses would find nearby delivery zone useful.
